ops.title: jj secondary workspaces (`jj workspace add`) are never git-colocated — git commands and ccx vcs fail there; verify live git behavior via a bookmark + local clone
ops.body:
Secondary jj workspaces created with `jj workspace add` have a `.jj` but no `.git`, even when the default workspace is colocated. Consequences (hit 2026-07-03 while building the anchors feature): `git status` fails with "not a git repository", and `ccx vcs diff`/anything shelling to git silently reports "No changes" or errors.

**Why:** colocation is a property of the default workspace only; jj does not replicate the git dir into added workspaces.

**How to apply:** implement/test Go code freely in the isolated workspace (`task build/test/lint` all work), but for live verification of git-backed surfaces: `jj bookmark create <name> -r @-`, `jj git export`, then `git clone --branch <name> <default-workspace-path> <scratch>` and run the binaries from the clone. Related: [[jj-shared-workingcopy-collision]] (the reason to use an added workspace in the first place — and note another session's commit can still land in your workspace's lineage; a later `jj rebase -b <branch> -d main --skip-emptied` dedupes it away if it reached main separately).
